role: A reusable React hook that measures a target DOM element's content-box dimensions and exposes them to consuming components.
capabilities: Attach a ref to a DOM element to measure it
Initialize width/height from clientWidth/clientHeight on mount
Observe content-box size changes via ResizeObserver
Expose current width and height as reactive state
Clean up the ResizeObserver on unmount
substance_why: The file contains a complete, typed hook implementation with ResizeObserver setup, initial measurement, state management, and cleanup, and it is explicitly shared by two views.
interactions_missing: No ResizeObserver fallback for unsupported browsers
No debouncing or throttling of rapid resize callbacks
No measurement before the effect runs (initial size is 0x0)
No handling of ref changes or element swaps after mount
performance_risks: ResizeObserver callbacks can fire frequently and trigger a React state update on every resize
Reading clientWidth/clientHeight in the effect can force synchronous layout
Size-driven renders could alter layout and feed back into the ResizeObserver, risking an observer→state→layout loop
reuse_why: The measurement concept is correct and needed by both wheel and separator views, but it should be hardened with throttling, fallback handling, and better initial-state behavior.
decomposition_hints: Move from wheel/ to a shared hooks directory because it is already used outside the wheel view
Optionally split a lower-level useResizeObserver primitive from the size-state convenience hook
